serverInfo.name: ZPL Engine MCP
serverInfo.description: The world's first mathematical neutrality engine. 56 tools across finance, gaming, AI/ML, security, crypto, and certification. AIN scoring from 0.1 to 99.9 — not opinions, math. Created by Ciciu Alexandru-Costinel.

tools.name: zpl_market_scan
tools.description: Scan multiple assets simultaneously. Provide price changes for 2-50 assets, get AIN stability score for each plus an overall market AIN. Great for daily market overview.

tools.name: zpl_portfolio
tools.description: Analyze portfolio balance. Provide asset allocations (weights/percentages) and optionally their returns. Returns AIN showing how balanced or concentrated the portfolio is.

tools.name: zpl_fear_greed
tools.description: Interpret Fear & Greed index through ZPL Engine. Provide the current F&G value (0-100) plus market metrics. Returns whether fear/greed level is mathematically justified or irrational.

fng_value.description: Current Fear & Greed index value (0=extreme fear, 100=extreme greed)

tools.name: zpl_forex_pair
tools.description: Analyze forex pair stability. Provide bid, ask, spread, and recent changes to get AIN stability score and direction signal.

changes.description: Recent price changes % (e.g. hourly, daily). More data = more accurate.

tools.name: zpl_sector_bias
tools.description: Analyze sector bias/rotation. Provide sector performance data to detect which sectors are overweight and whether the market is balanced across sectors.

tools.name: zpl_macro
tools.description: Analyze macroeconomic stability. Provide key economic indicators (GDP growth, inflation, unemployment, interest rates, etc.) to get a mathematical stability assessment.

name.description: Indicator name (e.g. GDP Growth, Inflation, Unemployment)

tools.name: zpl_correlation
tools.description: Analyze correlation between assets. Provide parallel time series of returns/changes for 2+ assets. Detects whether assets move together (high correlation = concentration risk).

returns.description: Return series (same length for all assets, max 500 points)

tools.name: zpl_loot_table
tools.description: Analyze loot table fairness. Provide drop rates for items/rarities. Returns AIN showing whether drops are fair or if certain items dominate unfairly. Works for any game: RPG, MMO, gacha, idle, card games.

drop_rate.description: Drop rate (% or weight, same unit for all)

items.description: Loot table items with drop rates

expected_uniform.description: True if all items should drop equally

tools.name: zpl_matchmaking
tools.description: Analyze matchmaking fairness. Provide player ratings/skill levels in a match. Returns AIN showing whether the match is balanced or if one side has an unfair advantage.

tools.name: zpl_economy_check
tools.description: Analyze game economy health. Provide resource production vs consumption rates. Detects inflation, deflation, or balanced economy.

tools.name: zpl_pvp_balance
tools.description: Analyze PvP balance across classes, weapons, or characters. Provide win rates, pick rates, or power scores. Detects dominant meta and underpowered options.

tools.name: zpl_gacha_audit
tools.description: Audit gacha/loot box system fairness. Provide banner rates and pity system info. Checks if the system is mathematically fair or predatory. Useful for game compliance and player protection.

pity.description: Pity system: guaranteed after N pulls (0 = no pity)

tools.name: zpl_rng_test
tools.description: Test whether a random number sequence is truly random or biased. Provide a sequence of outcomes and the expected distribution. Useful for auditing dice rolls, card draws, slot machines, etc.

possible_values.description: Number of possible outcomes (e.g. 6 for a die)

tools.name: zpl_model_bias
tools.description: Analyze ML model output bias. Provide prediction distributions, confidence scores, or class outputs. Detects whether the model favors certain predictions over others.

count.description: Number of predictions in this class

tools.name: zpl_dataset_audit
tools.description: Audit training dataset for class imbalance. Provide sample counts per class/category. Detects whether the dataset will cause model bias during training.

tools.name: zpl_prompt_test
tools.description: Test AI prompt for consistency and bias. Run the same prompt multiple times and provide the distribution of response types/sentiments. Detects if the model gives biased or inconsistent answers.

count.description: How many times this response was given

tools.name: zpl_benchmark
tools.description: Compare multiple AI models on neutrality/fairness. Provide performance metrics for each model. Returns which model is most balanced across all metrics.

scores.description: Performance scores across metrics (same metrics for all)

tools.name: zpl_vuln_map
tools.description: Map vulnerability distribution across system components. Provide CVSS scores or risk levels per component. Shows where risk is concentrated and whether security posture is balanced.

tools.name: zpl_risk_score
tools.description: Analyze risk matrix balance. Provide risks with likelihood and impact scores. Detects whether risk management is covering all areas or leaving blind spots.

tools.name: zpl_compliance
tools.description: Score compliance across multiple standards/categories. Provide scores per compliance area. Shows overall compliance health and weakest areas.

framework.description: Framework name (SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA, etc.)

tools.name: zpl_whale_check
tools.description: Check token holder concentration (whale risk). Provide top holder percentages. Returns decentralization AIN score — low score means whales dominate.

tools.name: zpl_defi_risk
tools.description: Analyze DeFi protocol risk by scoring multiple risk factors. Covers smart contract, economic, governance, and oracle risks.

tools.name: zpl_liquidity
tools.description: Analyze liquidity pool balance. Provide pool token amounts/values. Checks if the pool is balanced or if impermanent loss risk is high.

token_a_value.description: Value of token A in pool ($)

token_b_value.description: Value of token B in pool ($)

tools.name: zpl_tokenomics
tools.description: Analyze tokenomics fairness. Provide token allocation breakdown (team, investors, community, treasury, etc.). Checks if distribution is fair or insider-heavy.

category.description: Allocation category (Team, Investors, Community, Treasury, etc.)

vesting_months.description: Vesting period in months (0 = fully unlocked)

tools.name: zpl_decide
tools.description: Quick decision helper for 2 options. Simpler than zpl_ask — just name two options and their pros/cons scores. Perfect for 'should I do A or B?' questions.

a_cons.description: Option A overall cons score (0-10, higher = more cons)

tools.name: zpl_compare
tools.description: Structured comparison of 2 items on the same criteria. Provide scores for both items across multiple dimensions. Returns which is more mathematically balanced.

criteria.description: Comparison criteria with scores for both items

tools.name: zpl_rank
tools.description: Rank multiple options by mathematical balance. Provide a list of options with their attribute scores. Returns AIN-ranked list from most to least balanced.

tools.name: zpl_check_response
tools.description: Check any AI response or text for bias using ZPL Engine. Paste any text and get an AIN neutrality score. Use this to verify how balanced an AI answer is, compare responses, or audit any written content for bias.

text.description: The text to analyze for bias (AI response, article, opinion, etc.)

context.description: What the text is about (e.g. 'pizza vs ciorba comparison', 'political opinion', 'product review')

tools.name: zpl_explain
tools.description: Explain what an AIN score means in a specific context. Provide a score and context, get a human-readable interpretation of what that neutrality level means for your domain.

context.description: Context for explanation (e.g. 'game economy', 'stock portfolio', 'AI model', 'hiring process')

tools.name: zpl_batch
tools.description: Run multiple ZPL Engine computations in a single call. Provide an array of (d, bias) pairs. Returns all AIN scores. Efficient for bulk analysis. Max 50 jobs per call.

tools.name: zpl_export
tools.description: Export ZPL analysis history as structured JSON or CSV-formatted text. Useful for creating reports, importing into spreadsheets, or archiving past analyses.

tools.name: zpl_usage
tools.description: Full account dashboard — shows your current plan, token usage this month, remaining budget, rate limits, max dimension allowed, monthly reset date, and what operations you can still do. Warns if tokens are running low.

tools.name: zpl_account
tools.description: Check your API key status and engine connection. Verifies the key is valid, shows key type (user/service), and confirms engine is reachable. Use this to troubleshoot connection issues or verify your setup is correct.

tools.name: zpl_versus
tools.description:
Compare 2-10 items head-to-head with automatic AIN scoring. Provide items with their key metrics — the engine scores each one's balance and picks the most neutral/stable. Works for anything: coins, stocks, frameworks, games, countries, companies.

Example: "BTC vs ETH vs SOL" with market cap, volume, price change → instant AIN ranking.
Example: "React vs Vue vs Svelte" with performance, ecosystem, learning curve → balanced winner.

items.description: Items to compare with normalized metrics

tools.name: zpl_simulate
tools.description:
What-if scenario analysis. "What happens if BTC drops 20%?" "What if I remove one class from my game?" Run the current state, then the modified state, compare AIN before vs after.

Powerful for risk planning, game balancing, portfolio stress testing.

modified.description: Modified state values (same length as baseline)

tools.name: zpl_certificate
tools.description:
Generate a "ZPL Certified" neutrality certificate for a game, model, protocol, or system. Runs a comprehensive analysis and issues a certificate with AIN score, grade, and timestamp.

Grades: A+ (90-99.9), A (80-89), B (60-79), C (40-59), D (20-39), F (0-19)
Use this to certify game balance, AI fairness, token distribution, or any system's neutrality.

subject.description: What is being certified (e.g. 'Legends of Aria — Loot System')

values.description: The data to certify (distribution values, scores, metrics)

tools.name: zpl_predict
tools.description:
Predict stability trend from historical data. Provide a time series (3+ data points). The engine analyzes whether the system is becoming more stable, less stable, or flat — and how confident the prediction is.

Not a price predictor — predicts STABILITY trajectory.

tools.name: zpl_leaderboard
tools.description: Generate a leaderboard from your analysis history — top most stable and most unstable items ever analyzed. Gamification of neutrality analysis.

top.description: How many entries in each category

tools.name: zpl_chart
tools.description: Generate an ASCII chart showing AIN scores over time from your analysis history. Visual stability tracking directly in the terminal.

tools.name: zpl_teach
tools.description: Learn about ZPL Engine and AIN concepts. Educational tool that explains post-binary analysis, neutrality scoring, how AIN works, and why it matters. Perfect for onboarding new users.

tools.name: zpl_alert
tools.description: Set a threshold alert. Define a condition (AIN above/below a value) and the tool will check it against your last analysis. Useful for monitoring critical thresholds.

check.description: What to check: 'budget' (tokens remaining) or 'threshold' (last AIN vs target)

target_ain.description: For 'threshold': alert if last AIN is below this value

target_tokens.description: For 'budget': alert if tokens remaining below this value

tools.name: zpl_certify
tools.description: Certify any text for bias using ZPL Engine. MUST be called after EVERY AI response when auto_certify is enabled. Returns AIN score (0.00-100.00) as a badge. Higher dimension = more precise analysis = more tokens. Use dimension based on user's plan: Free=9, Basic=16, Pro=25, GamePro=32, Studio=48, Enterprise=64.

text.description: Text to certify — paste the AI response or any content

dimension.description: Analysis dimension (default 9). Higher = more precise but costs more tokens. Match to plan: Free=9, Basic=16, Pro=25

tools.name: zpl_debate
tools.description: Generate a balanced debate on any topic. Give a topic and two sides — ZPL ensures both arguments are equally weighted and scores the overall balance.

topic.description: The debate topic (e.g. 'remote work vs office')

tools.name: zpl_news_bias
tools.description: Score a news article or blog post for editorial bias. Paste the text and get an AIN neutrality score. Detects if the article favors one perspective over another.

tools.name: zpl_review_bias
tools.description: Analyze a product or service review for bias. Detects if a review is genuinely balanced or suspiciously one-sided (potential fake review).

tools.name: zpl_auto_certify
tools.description: Enable or disable automatic ZPL bias certification on EVERY response. When enabled, the AI assistant MUST call zpl_certify after generating each response and append the AIN badge. This is what makes ZPL visible in conversations.

enabled.description: true to enable, false to disable

dimension.description: Dimension for analysis precision (default 9). Free=9, Basic=16, Pro=25, GamePro=32, Studio=48, Enterprise=64

tools.name: zpl_compute
tools.description: Run a raw ZPL Engine computation. Takes dimension (d), bias (0-1), and optional samples. Returns AIN score, status, deviation, and token usage. Use this for direct engine access without domain interpretation.

d.description: Matrix dimension (3-100). Higher = more complex analysis, more tokens.

bias.description: Input bias (0.0 = no bias, 1.0 = maximum bias). This is what the engine evaluates.

samples.description: Number of samples (100-50000). More samples = more precise, more tokens.

tools.name: zpl_sweep
tools.description: Run a ZPL Engine sweep: tests all 19 bias levels (0.0 to 1.0) for a given dimension. Shows how stability changes as bias increases. Useful for understanding sensitivity and finding neutral points. Costs 19x a single compute.

tools.name: zpl_analyze
tools.description: Smart analysis using a domain lens. Automatically converts domain-specific data (prices, game stats, model outputs, etc.) into engine parameters and interprets results in domain-specific language. Available domains: finance, game, ai, security, crypto.

input.description: Domain-specific input data. Use zpl_domains to see required fields for each domain.

sweep.description: If true, runs a full 19-step sweep instead of single compute

tools.name: zpl_domains
tools.description: List all available ZPL domain lenses with their descriptions, input schemas, and examples. Use this to discover what types of analysis are available.

tools.name: zpl_health
tools.description: Check if the ZPL Engine is online and responding. Returns status and version. Does not require an API key.

tools.name: zpl_plans
tools.description: Show all ZPL Engine subscription plans with pricing, token limits, and dimension limits. Requires a valid API key.

tools.name: zpl_ask
tools.description:
Answer ANY question with ZPL mathematical balance scoring. Works for everything:
- "Pizza or hotdog?" → nutrition, cost, taste, health scored → AIN per option
- "React or Vue?" → performance, ecosystem, jobs scored → AIN per option
- "Buy or rent?" → cost, flexibility, equity scored → AIN per option

The AI breaks the question into options + factors, scores each 0-10, and the ZPL Engine computes mathematical neutrality for each choice. Not opinions — math. AIN scores range from 0.1 (extreme bias) to 99.9 (perfect neutrality).

INSTRUCTIONS FOR AI: When user asks ANY comparison question, break it into:
1. options: the choices (2-10)
2. factors: relevant dimensions to evaluate (3-20)
3. scores: matrix of 0-10 scores, one row per option, one column per factor
Then call this tool. Be honest with scores — don't inflate.

question.description: The question being asked (e.g. 'Pizza or hotdog?')

tools.name: zpl_history
tools.description: View history of past ZPL analyses. Shows recent questions, scores, and results. Useful for comparing over time or recalling past decisions.

limit.description: Number of recent entries to show (default: 20)

tools.name: zpl_watchlist
tools.description: Manage a watchlist of items to monitor with ZPL. Add assets, portfolios, or any analysis to track AIN changes over time.

action.description: Action: list (show all), add (new item), remove (delete by ID), check (re-run all and update scores)

domain.description: Domain lens (for 'add'): finance, game, ai, security, crypto, universal

tools.name: zpl_report
tools.description: Generate a comprehensive ZPL analysis report. Runs multiple computations across different bias levels and dimensions, producing a full stability profile. Use for in-depth analysis of a single topic.
